plan.title: S1: content dependency + TS resolve-and-copy deposit primitive

narratives.Description: directive init/update currently obtain framework content only via the Go binary's GitHub-tarball download, and @deftai/directive depends solely on @deftai/directive-core. This story adds @deftai/directive-content as a dependency of the engine package and builds a pure TS deposit primitive that resolves the installed content package via Node module resolution and recursively copies its tree into a target directory with file-mode preservation. It is the foundation every later init/update story builds on, and is independently buildable and testable in isolation from the CLI verbs.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Add @deftai/directive-content (workspace:*) to packages/cli/package.json dependencies alongside @deftai/directive-core.
- Implement packages/core/src/deposit/resolve-content.ts using import.meta.resolve('@deftai/directive-content') with a graceful error when the package is unresolvable, and packages/core/src/deposit/copy-tree.ts that recursively copies a source tree preserving the executable bit (mirroring cmd/deft-install copyFile/copyDir #1477).
- Unit-test resolution success/failure and a copy round-trip (including mode preservation and nested dirs) in colocated *.test.ts files.
narratives.UserStory: As a Deft engine package, I want to resolve and copy the installed content package tree locally, so that init/update can deposit .deft/core from npm without any network download.

items.title: When pnpm install runs, it resolves @deftai/directive-content as a dependency of the engine package without error.

narrative.Acceptance: When pnpm install runs, it resolves @deftai/directive-content as a dependency of the engine package without error.

items.title: resolve-content.ts returns the resolved content package root and rejects an uninstalled package with an actionable error.

narrative.Acceptance: resolve-content.ts returns the resolved content package root and rejects an uninstalled package with an actionable error.

items.title: copy-tree.ts creates a recursive copy of the source tree preserving the executable bit and nested directories.

narrative.Acceptance: copy-tree.ts creates a recursive copy of the source tree preserving the executable bit and nested directories.

items.title: When the unit tests run, they cover resolution success, resolution failure, and a copy round-trip that preserves file modes.

narrative.Acceptance: When the unit tests run, they cover resolution success, resolution failure, and a copy round-trip that preserves file modes.

expected_outputs: resolve-content + copy-tree primitives exported with passing unit tests
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to cmd/deft-install/setup.go (copyFile/copyDir, #1477) and the #1942 deposit-mechanism comment (resolve-and-copy), not a spec section.
